title: Cynoglossum rechingeri
intro: Cynoglossum rechingeri, commonly known as Rechinger's hound's tongue, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Tibetan and Himalayan cultures, for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum rechingeri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, or used in combination with other herbs in various formulations, such as capsules or tablets.
taxonomy: Cynoglossum rechingeri is a species of the plant genus Cynoglossum, which belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This family is further classified under the order Boraginales and class Magnoliopsida. Within the family, the genus Cynoglossum is grouped into the tribe Cynoglosseae. This species is also known to be closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Cynoglossum amabilis and Cynoglossum elegans. Its classification is based on its unique characteristics, including its leaf structure and flower morphology.
